POL.Name: neg_vn
POL.Comment: DEF: Recode negative incomes to zero

POL.Name: bsacv01_vn
POL.Comment: BEN: COVID benefit for poor and nearly poor households

POL.Name: poa01_vn
POL.Comment: BEN: Pension benefit for poor older people living without family support (i.e. living without anyone of working age)

POL.Name: bsacv02_vn
POL.Comment: BEN: COVID benefit for households with businesses and low business incomes

POL.Name: xhhadj_vn
POL.Comment: DEF: Adjust consumption to new disposable income

POL.Name: SetDefault_vn
POL.Comment: DEF: Default values for switched-off policies

FUN.Name: DefTu
FUN.Comment: A second definition household of household, with head defined as person with highest earnings; needed for tax rebate allocation

FUN.Name: SchedCalc
FUN.Comment: Tax schedule for personal income tax

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Define tax base for labour income tax

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Set dependent deduction to zero if the household member with the highest earnings is not a formal sector worker

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Provide benefits based on eligibility criteria

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Random number for those with positive COVID benefits

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Downward adjust benefits based on actual receipt

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Taxes (“Direct taxes” in Statistics Presenter)”

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Employee social insurance contributions (needed for Summary Statistics Plug In)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Self-employed social insurance contributions (not relevant but needed for Summary Stats Plugin)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Employer social insurance contributions (not relevant but needed for Summary Stats Plugin)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Disposable income not accounting for own produce

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Post-fiscal disposable income not accounting for own produce

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Post-fiscal disposable income not accounting for own produce

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Indirect taxes (part of "indirect taxes minus indirect benefits” in Statistics Presenter)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Social security contributions ("SSC (employer and employee)" in Statistics Presenter)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Child benefits ("Child benefits" in Statistics Presenter)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Social assistance benefits ("Social assistance benefits" in Statistics Presenter)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Agricultural benefits ("Agricultural benefits" in Statistics Presenter)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Widows and Orphans Benefits needed for Stats Presenter

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Disabled benefits ("Disabled benefits" in Statistics Presenter)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Unemployment benefit ("Unemployment benefits" in Statistics Presenter)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Simulated consumption net of indirect taxes (needed for Statistics Presenter - do not change)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Disposable income accounting for imputed own produce if appropriate (needed for Statistics Presenter - do not change)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Simulated Consumption (needed for Statistics Presenter - do not change)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list -Post-fiscal income accounting for imputed own produce if appropriate (needed for Statistics Presenter - do not change)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Define income list - Indirect benefits (part of "indirect taxes minus indirect benefits” in Statistics Presenter)

FUN.Name: ArithOp
FUN.Comment: Calculate total VAT paid on standard rated items by each household at 5%

FUN.Name: ArithOp
FUN.Comment: Calculate VAT rate on standard rated items at 10%, not including excise duty items

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: List of items for which VAT rate is 10%

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: List of items for which VAT rate is 5%

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: List of items for which VAT rate is 8% in 2022-24

FUN.Name: ArithOp
FUN.Comment: Calculate VAT rate on 10% items to which the reduced 8% rate applies in 2022 and 2023, not including excise duty items

FUN.Name: ArithOp
FUN.Comment: Excise: Alcohol of various kinds (excluding beer)

FUN.Name: ArithOp
FUN.Comment: Vat: Alcohol of various kinds (excluding beer)

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Lower poverty line for URBAN/RURAL households

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Lower POST FISCAL Poverty line for URBAN/RURAL households

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Upper poverty line for URBAN/RURAL households

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Upper POST FISCAL Poverty line for URBAN/RURAL households

FUN.Name: ArithOp
FUN.Comment: Per Capita Equivalence Scale (ses01) or Square Root Equivalence Scale (ses02)

FUN.Name: ArithOp
FUN.Comment: Calculate HH level sum of 'business/self-employment income'

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Calculate benefits based on eligibility criteria

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Random number for those with positive COVID benefits

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Downward adjust benefits based on actual receipt

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Random allocation of workers into unemployment (requires that random number < transition share for the relevant sector)

FUN.Name: DefConst
FUN.Comment: Specify 'transition shares' (shares of employed workers transitioning to unemployment across sectors) and a constant for the share of own-account production consumed that is protected from the shock

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Remove the industry value if the above criteria are not met

FUN.Name: Elig
FUN.Comment: Set eligibility criteria for labour market transitions

FUN.Name: DefConst
FUN.Comment: Specify share of own-account production consumed that is protected from changes

FUN.Name: Elig
FUN.Comment: Proxy for poor list: urban (lower poverty line)

FUN.Name: Elig
FUN.Comment: Proxy for poor list: rural (lower poverty line)

FUN.Name: Elig
FUN.Comment: Proxy for poor list: rural (upper poverty line)

FUN.Name: Elig
FUN.Comment: Proxy for poor list: urban (upper poverty line)

FUN.Name: ArithOp
FUN.Comment: Diesel oil, litres consumed per household

FUN.Name: DefConst
FUN.Comment: Retail prices and reference fuel prices

FUN.Name: ArithOp
FUN.Comment: Motor gasoline/petroleum, litres consumed per household

FUN.Name: DefVar
FUN.Comment: Initiate quantity variables (litres of each fuel consumed)

PAR.Name: WarnIfNoFactor
PAR.Comment: Warn if no uprating factor for a monetary variable

PAR.Name: x1390
PAR.Comment: 400. Fees and charges on administrative and legal services for daily-life requirements (certificates of marriage, birth, and death, notary services…)

PAR.Name: x131204
PAR.Comment: 220. Skin-nourishing cream, powder and lipsticks, perfume, hair gel,..

PAR.Name: x111123
PAR.Comment: 1532. Meals and drinks of household members working, studying, health treatmenting away from home

PAR.Name: x111122
PAR.Comment: 1531. Breakfast, lunch, dinner of members living in the household

PAR.Name: x111121
PAR.Comment: 153. Outdoors meals and drinks? (breakfast, lunch, dinner)

PAR.Name: x08310
PAR.Comment: 327. Fees of phone subscription, calls, and repairs

PAR.Name: x07300
PAR.Comment: 321. Travel expenditure (including fees for boats, ferries, and others)

PAR.Name: x07212
PAR.Comment: 319. Inner tubes, tires, and spare parts of scooters, automobiles

PAR.Name: x07211
PAR.Comment: 318. Inner tubes, tires, and spare parts of bicycles

PAR.Name: x06310
PAR.Comment: 329. Expenditures on plastic (cosmetic) surgeries, gym exercises

PAR.Name: x0562
PAR.Comment: 320. Maintenance and repair of household utensils

PAR.Name: x05522
PAR.Comment: 315. Torches, batteries for lighting, TVs, radio

PAR.Name: x05401
PAR.Comment: 311. Ceramics and glassware: bowls, plates, teapots, cups,...

PAR.Name: x05321
PAR.Comment: 310. Electrical appliances: light bulbs, electrical cords, plugs, fuses,...

PAR.Name: x05220
PAR.Comment: 307. Fees of tailoring, washing and ironing

PAR.Name: x0521
PAR.Comment: 305. Mats, blankets, bed sheets, pillows, curtains, table-cloth

PAR.Name: x045423
PAR.Comment: 212. Farm by-products (straw, sugar cane leaves, maize/jute/hemp/seagrass stems,…)

PAR.Name: x031312
PAR.Comment: 309. Plastics, conical hats, hats, umbrellas of various kinds

PAR.Name: x031311
PAR.Comment: 304. handkerchiefs, turbans, scarfs of various kinds

PAR.Name: x02190
PAR.Comment: 146. Bottled, canned, boxed beverages (pure water, carbonated drinks, juice, fruit smoothies, pep drinks,...)

PAR.Name: x01199
PAR.Comment: 154. Other food and drinks (other processed food and foodstuff, additives, seasonings, ...)

PAR.Name: x011752
PAR.Comment: 105. Potato of various kinds (in fresh-type equivalent)

PAR.Name: x01174
PAR.Comment: 130. Other vegetables (gourd, winter melon, cucumber, cabbage, squash...)

PAR.Name: x011613
PAR.Comment: 134. Other fruits (rambutan melon, papaya, guava, litchi, grapes,...)

PAR.Name: x01148
PAR.Comment: 121. Eggs of chickens, ducks, Muscovy ducks, geese

PAR.Name: x011342
PAR.Comment: 120. Other aquatic products and seafood (crabs, snails,...)

PAR.Name: x01125
PAR.Comment: 116. Processed meat (boiled pork pies, fried pork pies, roasted pork, sausages,...)

PAR.Name: x011226
PAR.Comment: 115. Other types of meat (goats, dogs, sheep, wild animals, birds,...)

PAR.Name: x011221
PAR.Comment: 110. Pork (in equivalent of the pork type with removed fat)

PAR.Name: x011152
PAR.Comment: 108. Fresh rice noodle, dried rice noodle

PAR.Name: x011111
PAR.Comment: 101. Plain rice (including fragrant and specialty rice)

PAR.Name: $pline_urban
PAR.Comment: Poverty line for urban areas (for 2014, the Government's 2011-15 poverty line, 2011-15; for 2016-19, Government's income-based indicators; for other years, uprated by CPI)

PAR.Name: $pline_rural
PAR.Comment: Poverty line for rural areas (for 2014, the Government's 2011-15 poverty line, 2011-15; for 2016-19, Government's income-based indicators; for other years, uprated by CPI)

PAR.Name: $pline_rural_pf
PAR.Comment: Post-fiscal poverty line for rural areas

PAR.Name: $pline_urban_pf
PAR.Comment: Post-fiscal poverty line for urban areas

PAR.Name: $bed01_amount
PAR.Comment: Benefit amount per eligible pupil for educational expenses

PAR.Name: $bot01_amount
PAR.Comment: Benefit amount for electricity subsidy (equivalent to 30kWh in the first bracket; calculated based on electricity prices multiplied by 30 days)

PAR.Name: $poa01_amount_aged_over_80
PAR.Comment: Benefit amount for poor older persons living alone: aged over 80 (2*270 until 2020, 2*360 from 2021)

PAR.Name: $poa01_amount_aged_60_80
PAR.Comment: Benefit amount for poor older persons living alone: aged 60-80 (1*270 until 2020, 1.5*360 from 2021)

PAR.Name: $bsacv01_amount
PAR.Comment: Benefit amount for people receiving person-level financial relief package for poor and nearly poor households

PAR.Name: $pline_rural_up
PAR.Comment: Income threshold for poor and near-poor in rural areas (in 2013-21 using the 2016-20 multi-dimensional poverty line; from 2022 onwards using the 2021-25 multidimensional poverty line; both uprated by CPI when needed)

PAR.Name: $pline_urban_up
PAR.Comment: Income threshold for poor and near-poor in urban areas (in 2013-21 using the 2016-20 multi-dimensional poverty line; from 2022 onwards using the 2021-25 multidimensional poverty line; both uprated by CPI when needed)

PAR.Name: $bsacv02_amount
PAR.Comment: Benefit amount for household businesses receiving COVID support

PAR.Name: $bsacv02_income_threshold
PAR.Comment: Income threshold for household businesses applied in eligibilty rules for COVID support policy

PAR.Name: $pline_urban_pf_up
PAR.Comment: Upper post-fiscal poverty line for urban areas

PAR.Name: $pline_rural_pf_up
PAR.Comment: Upper post-fiscal poverty line for rural areas

PAR.Name: $vat_r3
PAR.Comment: Reduced 8% VAT rate applied to all applicable expenditure items in 2022 and 2023 that were originally taxed at 10%

PAR.Name: $poa01_amount_aged_75_80_ethnic
PAR.Comment: Benefit amount for poor older persons NOT living alone of ethnic population: aged over 75-80 (1*360 from 2021)

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: Gross employment income times the SSNIT rate for employer contributions

PAR.Name: comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: Gross employment income times the SSNIT rate for employee contributions

PAR.Name: formula
PAR.Comment: Investment and rental income times the capital income tax rate

PAR.Name: output_var
PAR.Comment: Tax income from investment income and rental income (i.e. interest)

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: A child (18 or below), with no earnings

PAR.Name: TAX_UNIT
PAR.Comment: Assigned to the household member with the highest earnings

PAR.Name: Formula
PAR.Comment: Tax base minus total tax deduction

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: Social contributions are deducted from labour earnings to form the tax base

PAR.Name: Output_Var
PAR.Comment: Record to a variable for tax base before deductions

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: If not formal sector worker (household head) but deduction was calculated to be positive

PAR.Name: Output_Add_Var
PAR.Comment: Record value (0), replacing the original value

PAR.Name: TAX_UNIT
PAR.Comment: Assigned to the household member with the highest earnings

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Individuals living in households with per capita incomes below the poverty line threshold

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: Default position is that the benefit is not included, as it was not active on 1st July 2020

PAR.Name: TAX_UNIT
PAR.Comment: Each individual can receive the benefit

PAR.Name: #_Level
PAR.Comment: Per capita income calculated at HH level

PAR.Name: #_Level
PAR.Comment: Per capita income calculated at HH level

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Individuals living in households with per capita incomes below the poverty line threshold

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: Note: Switch 'ON' to specify that benefit is only paid for the actual 3 months (lump sum)

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Note: Adjustment is 0.353 in the VN COVID study, using 2018 data. Here we use 2020 data, with lower poverty, less recipients, and therefore more limited over simulation of benefits.

PAR.Name: Elig_Cond
PAR.Comment: Calculate the number of people aged between 16 and 59 in each household

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Benefit for poor older persons living alone: aged 60-80

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Benefit amount for poor older persons living alone: aged over 80

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Benefit amount for poor older persons NOT living alone of ethnic origin

PAR.Name: Name
PAR.Comment: Non-means-tested cash benefits – excluding pension-related income (as in ils_pen)

PAR.Name: Name
PAR.Comment: Means-tested cash benefits – excluding pension-related income (as in ils_pen)

PAR.Name: bsacv01_s
PAR.Comment: Simulated benefit for COVID response policy targetted at poor or nearly poor individuals @ VND 250,000 per person per month for duration of 3 months

PAR.Name: bsacv02_s
PAR.Comment: Simulated benefit for COVID response targetted at household businesses

PAR.Name: Name
PAR.Comment: Composed of all pension benefits, means-tested cash benefits, and non-means-tested cash benefits (ils_pen + ils_benmt + ils_bennt)

PAR.Name: ils_ben
PAR.Comment: Simulated Benefits plus non sim Benefits in Original Data Set

PAR.Name: Name
PAR.Comment: Composed of all contribution-based pension benefits and non-contributory social pension benefits, simulated or not

PAR.Name: poa01_s
PAR.Comment: Simulated benefit for pension benefit paid to older persons living alone

PAR.Name: ils_dispy
PAR.Comment: Disposable income not accounting for own produce (as above)

PAR.Name: ils_dispy
PAR.Comment: Disposable income not accounting for own produce (as above)

PAR.Name: bsacv01_s
PAR.Comment: Simulated benefit for COVID response policy targetted at poor or nearly poor individuals @ VND 250,000 per person per month for duration of 3 months

PAR.Name: bsacv02_s
PAR.Comment: Simulated benefit for COVID response targetted at household businesses

PAR.Name: xivot
PAR.Comment: Imputed value of own produce (food and non-food)

PAR.Name: xhh_s
PAR.Comment: Consumption adjusted by difference between disposable income in current and base year

PAR.Name: Name
PAR.Comment: Disposable income net of indirect taxes

PAR.Name: ils_dispyx
PAR.Comment: Disposable income accounting for own produce

PAR.Name: x0130
PAR.Comment: Services for processing primary goods for food and non-alcoholic beverages (S)

PAR.Name: x0123
PAR.Comment: Tea, maté and other plant products for infusion (ND)

PAR.Name: x0119
PAR.Comment: Ready-made food and other food products n.e.c. (ND)

PAR.Name: x0117
PAR.Comment: Vegetables, tubers, plantains, cooking bananas and pulses (ND)

PAR.Name: x0114
PAR.Comment: Milk, other dairy products and eggs (ND)

PAR.Name: x0112
PAR.Comment: Live animals, meat and other parts of slaughtered land animals (ND)

PAR.Name: x056
PAR.Comment: Goods and services for routine household maintenance

PAR.Name: x055
PAR.Comment: Tools and equipment for house and garden

PAR.Name: x044
PAR.Comment: Water supply and miscellaneous services relating to the dwelling

PAR.Name: x043
PAR.Comment: Maintenance, repair and security of the dwelling

PAR.Name: Formula
PAR.Comment: Calculate total VAT paid on standard rated items by each household at 5%

PAR.Name: Formula
PAR.Comment: Calculate total VAT paid on standard rated items by each household at 10%

PAR.Name: Name
PAR.Comment: NB. zero-rated or exempt items should be reclassified as n/a, including those to which VAT is applied in the Excise Duty policy

PAR.Name: x1390
PAR.Comment: 400. Fees and charges on administrative and legal services for daily-life requirements (certificates of marriage, birth, and death, notary services…)

PAR.Comment: 220. Skin-nourishing cream, powder and lipsticks, perfume, hair gel,..

PAR.Name: x05220
PAR.Comment: 307. Fees of tailoring, washing and ironing

PAR.Name: x05321
PAR.Comment: 310. Electrical appliances: light bulbs, electrical cords, plugs, fuses,...

PAR.Name: x05401
PAR.Comment: 311. Ceramics and glassware: bowls, plates, teapots, cups,...

PAR.Name: x05522
PAR.Comment: 315. Torches, batteries for lighting, TVs, radio

PAR.Name: x0562
PAR.Comment: 320. Maintenance and repair of household utensils

PAR.Name: x06310
PAR.Comment: 329. Expenditures on plastic (cosmetic) surgeries, gym exercises

PAR.Name: x07211
PAR.Comment: 318. Inner tubes, tires, and spare parts of bicycles

PAR.Name: x07212
PAR.Comment: 319. Inner tubes, tires, and spare parts of scooters, automobiles

PAR.Name: x07300
PAR.Comment: 321. Travel expenditure (including fees for boats, ferries, and others)

PAR.Name: x08310
PAR.Comment: 327. Fees of phone subscription, calls, and repairs

PAR.Name: x09470
PAR.Comment: 226. Lottery tickets - VAT APPLIED SEPARATELY WITH EXCISE TAX POLICY

PAR.Name: x09610
PAR.Comment: 330. Entertainment (cinemas, music, video, sports) - VAT APPLIED SEPARATELY WITH EXCISE TAX POLICY

PAR.Name: x111121
PAR.Comment: 153. Outdoors meals and drinks? (breakfast, lunch, dinner)

PAR.Name: x111122
PAR.Comment: 1531. Breakfast, lunch, dinner of members living in the household

PAR.Name: x111123
PAR.Comment: 1532. Meals and drinks of household members working, studying, health treatmenting away from home

PAR.Name: x131204
PAR.Comment: 220. Skin-nourishing cream, powder and lipsticks, perfume, hair gel,..

PAR.Name: x1390
PAR.Comment: 400. Fees and charges on administrative and legal services for daily-life requirements (certificates of marriage, birth, and death, notary services…)

PAR.Name: Formula
PAR.Comment: Calculate VAT rate on 10% items to which the reduced 8% rate applies in 2022 and 2023, not including excise duty items

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Lower poverty line for URBAN households

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Lower poverty line for RURAL households

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Lower POST FISCAL Poverty line for URBAN households

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Lower POST FISCAL Poverty line for RURAL households

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Upper poverty line for URBAN households

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Upper poverty line for RURAL households

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Upper POST FISCAL Poverty line for URBAN households

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Upper POST FISCAL Poverty line for RURAL households

PAR.Name: Formula
PAR.Comment: Can set to ses01 or ses02

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: Covers support for the purchase of text books, notebooks and other school supplies to disabled pupil, orphan pupil, and pupil of poor HH

PAR.Name: Elig_Cond
PAR.Comment: Househoold deemed to have a business if any one adult reports being 'business owner or self-employed'

PAR.Name: Who_Must_Be_Elig
PAR.Comment: HH must contain at least one adult in les=2

PAR.Name: Output_Var
PAR.Comment: Flag is created at HH level and assigned to HH head

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: HH receives benefit if has a business and has a combined yse income of less than the specified amount

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: Default (switch off) is that the benefit is NOT received as it was not active on 1st July 2020

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Switch 'on': the benefit is limited to 3 months, as per the true benefit rules.

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Note: Adjustment is 0.037 in both the VN COVID study, using 2018 data, and here when we use 2020 data.

PAR.Name: i_lma_elig
PAR.Comment: Variable denoting eligibility for labour market transitions

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: No change in labour market status...

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: No change in labour market status...

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: No change in labour market status...

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Transition to unemployment with zero income (if in the agricultural sector)

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: No change in labour market status...

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: No change in labour market status...

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: No change in labour market status...

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: If the individual meets these conditions...

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: ...industry value is retained (the individual is eligible for transition)

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: ...industry value is set to -1 (unknown; the individual is not eligible for transition)

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: If the individual does not meet these conditions...

PAR.Name: Elig_Cond
PAR.Comment: To have an industry value and to be eligible for a transition, les (labour market status) has to be equal to 1, 2 or 3 (employee or farmer) and non-farm earnings must be positive in sectors other than agriculture. In agriculture, those (household heads) with positive farm income may only transition if they have other sources of earnings and are in the formal sector.

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: No change in labour market status...

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: No change in labour market status...

PAR.Name: $protect_cons
PAR.Comment: An optional constant for the share of own-account production consumed that is protected from changes. This is ca. 25% in Sub-Saharan Africa, but can be set to 0%.

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Disposable income higher than in base year

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Disposable income lower than in base year

PAR.Name: i_poor_hh_rural_up
PAR.Comment: Rural poor household (upper poverty line, poor or near-poor households)

PAR.Name: i_poor_hh_urban_up
PAR.Comment: Urban poor household (upper poverty line, poor or near-poor households)

PAR.Name: Formula
PAR.Comment: Original income per household divided by household members

PAR.Name: Elig_Cond
PAR.Comment: Per capita income below the urban poverty line for households in urban areas

PAR.Name: Output_Var
PAR.Comment: Indicator for an individual in poor urban household

PAR.Name: Elig_Cond
PAR.Comment: Per capita income below the rural poverty line for households in rural areas

PAR.Name: Output_Var
PAR.Comment: Indicator for an individual in poor rural household

PAR.Name: Elig_Cond
PAR.Comment: Per capita income below the rural poverty line for households in rural areas

PAR.Name: Output_Var
PAR.Comment: Indicator for an individual in poor rural household

PAR.Name: Elig_Cond
PAR.Comment: Per capita income below the urban poverty line for households in urban areas

PAR.Name: Output_Var
PAR.Comment: Indicator for an individual in poor urban household

PAR.Name: Formula
PAR.Comment: Subsidy = Litres consumed * (retail price per litre - reference price per litre)

PAR.Name: Formula
PAR.Comment: Litres consumed = Expenditure / Price per litre

PAR.Name: $diesel_price
PAR.Comment: Automotive diesel, thousands VND per litre (government-set retail price)

PAR.Name: $gasoline_price
PAR.Comment: Motor gasoline, thousands VND per litre (government-set retail price)

PAR.Name: $kerosene_price
PAR.Comment: Kerosene, thousands VND per litre (government-set retail price)

PAR.Name: $diesel_ref
PAR.Comment: Automotive diesel, thousands VND per litre (reference price)

PAR.Name: $gasoline_ref
PAR.Comment: Motor gasoline, thousands VND per litre (reference price)

PAR.Name: $kerosene_ref
PAR.Comment: Kerosene, thousands VND per litre (reference price)

PAR.Name: Formula
PAR.Comment: Litres consumed = Expenditure / Price per litre

PAR.Name: Formula
PAR.Comment: Litres consumed = Expenditure / Price per litre

PAR.Name: Formula
PAR.Comment: Subsidy = Litres consumed * (retail price per litre - reference price per litre)

PAR.Name: Formula
PAR.Comment: Subsidy = Litres consumed * (retail price per litre - reference price per litre)

SYS_PAR.Value: tex01_s + tex02_s + tex03_s + tex04_s + tex05_s + tex06_s
tex01_s + tex02_s + tex03_s + tex04_s + tex05_s + tex06_s
tex01_s + tex02_s + tex03_s + tex04_s + tex05_s + tex06_s
tex01_s + tex02_s + tex03_s + tex04_s + tex05_s + tex06_s
tex01_s + tex02_s + tex03_s + tex04_s + tex05_s + tex06_s
tex01_s + tex02_s + tex03_s + tex04_s + tex05_s + tex06_s
tex01_s + tex02_s + tex03_s + tex04_s + tex05_s + tex06_s
tex01_s + tex02_s + tex03_s + tex04_s + tex05_s + tex06_s
(les=1 | les=2 | les=3) & ( (i_lindi01!=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) ) | (i_lindi01=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) & (dhh=1 & ( yag=0 | (yag>0 & lfo=1) ) ) ) )
tex01_s + tex02_s + tex03_s + tex04_s + tex05_s + tex06_s
(les=1 | les=2 | les=3) & ( (i_lindi01!=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) ) | (i_lindi01=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) & (dhh=1 & ( yag=0 | (yag>0 & lfo=1) ) ) ) )
tex01_s + tex02_s + tex03_s + tex04_s + tex05_s + tex06_s
(les=1 | les=2 | les=3) & ( (i_lindi01!=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) ) | (i_lindi01=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) & (dhh=1 & ( yag=0 | (yag>0 & lfo=1) ) ) ) )
tex01_s + tex02_s + tex03_s + tex04_s + tex05_s + tex06_s
(les=1 | les=2 | les=3) & ( (i_lindi01!=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) ) | (i_lindi01=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) & (dhh=1 & ( yag=0 | (yag>0 & lfo=1) ) ) ) )
tex01_s + tex02_s + tex03_s + tex04_s + tex05_s + tex06_s
(les=1 | les=2 | les=3) & ( (i_lindi01!=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) ) | (i_lindi01=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) & (dhh=1 & ( yag=0 | (yag>0 & lfo=1) ) ) ) )
